required.reason: Required after all three unchanged upstream assertions pass in native and forced-portable runs: initial checkbox/radio/option state, dynamic input type removal, and programmatic checkbox/radio/option activation. Independent V8 identity, event-order, native raster, geometry, hit-testing, and state spikes preserve the operated component-control coverage. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-checked-required-native-final/results.json and TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-checked-required-portable-final/results.json.

Required after exact native and forced-portable reftest passes. Because both WPT sides use native markers, required status remains qualified by the independent green R5 native marker pixel gate; the reftest alone could false-pass if both sides omitted marker paint.

Required after exact native and forced-portable reftest passes. The independent green R5 native marker pixel gate remains part of the evidence because marker omission on both reftest sides could otherwise mask a failure.

Required after exact native and forced-portable reftest passes. Required status remains coupled to the independent green marker geometry/raster and initial-font-metrics authorities so a test/reference marker omission or shared font fallback cannot create a false pass.

Required after exact native and forced-portable reftest passes. Independent native/portable geometry and V8 authored-text regressions retain coverage for the hypothetical inline insertion point and whitespace semantics exercised by the WPT.

Required after the unchanged two-subtest WPT passes in exact native and forced-portable runs. Required status remains coupled to the independent green local ancestor-bubbling spike and a high-DPI connected-component focus/highlight gate because this WPT listens on each focused target rather than an ancestor.

Required after the unchanged upstream keyboard-modality test passes in exact native and forced-portable runs. Required status remains coupled to the independent Avalonia NavigationMethod.Tab authority and an operated connected-menu gate; the bounded runner adapter supports only the WebDriver Tab action exercised here. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-focus-visible-cssom-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after all 12 unchanged upstream pointer-focus subtests pass in exact native and forced-portable runs across tabindex spans, button, image/reset/submit, checkbox, radio, range, and color inputs. Required status remains coupled to the independent modality authority and an operated connected-menu gate; the bounded runner adapter supports the primary click used here. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-focus-visible-cssom-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after the settled unchanged XHTML reference passes exact pixels in optimized native and forced-portable runs. This replaces the earlier false pass from a prematurely captured blank reference and is coupled to independent positioned-control geometry gates. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-candidate-post-focus-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after all four unchanged upstream assertions pass in optimized native and forced-portable runs: author !important survives inline add, modification, and removal. The run also exercises browser-compatible window named-property lookup. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-candidate-post-focus-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after exact native and forced-portable passes of the unchanged reftest. Vertical percentage-bearing calc() relative insets correctly become auto when the containing-block height is indefinite, while the independent authority retains definite and negative-length coverage. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-relative-inset-fixed-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after both unchanged upstream hit assertions pass in native and forced-portable runs. getBoundingClientRect uses transformed corner extents and elementFromPoint inverse-transforms the viewport point into the local border box, coupled to an independent scaled geometry/half-open edge authority. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-transform-hittest-fixed-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream assertion passes in exact native and forced-portable runs. Computed style serializes the applied transform matrix, while independent Chrome-authority regressions preserve authored noncommutative transform-list order in Avalonia's row-vector model. Evidence: TestResults/WPT-transform-order-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream negative-translation assertion passes in exact native and forced-portable runs. The same computed matrix is used for visual application and CSSOM serialization, coupled to independent ordered-composition tests. Evidence: TestResults/WPT-transform-order-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream reftest passes exact pixels in native and forced-portable runs. Fixed row and column tracks, gap, source-order auto placement, and collapsible authored whitespace are coupled to an independent V8 iframe geometry/raster authority. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-grid-rows-final-{native,portable}/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream block generated-content reftest passes exact pixels in native and forced-portable runs. Literal pseudo text is measured as intrinsic content, participates in normal flow, and paints through a DOM-invisible carrier without entering the DOM or flex item list. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-generated-current-{native,portable}-block/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream generated-pseudo flex reftest passes exact pixels in native and forced-portable runs. Literal before/after text preserves source ordering, intrinsic sizing, align-self, background paint, and anonymous authored text behavior; independent spikes retain native/portable geometry and raster proof. Evidence: TestResults/WebPlatformSubset-generated-current-{native,portable}-flex/results.json.

Required after the unchanged upstream positioned generated-content reftest passes exact pixels in native and forced-portable runs. Out-of-flow generated backgrounds use the shared containing-block resolver, current-pass layout slots, and the precomputed portable absolute containing block, coupled to an isolated live ICB geometry/raster authority.

candidate.reason: The actual flex document now matches the WPT-encoded margin, per-line shrink, overflow, and cross-line stretch geometry in native and forced-portable layout; their actual PNGs are byte-identical. Exact comparison remains blocked by unsupported construction on the reference side: ordinary block children still receive their left margin twice at the Avalonia arrange boundary, and the second of two exact-fit floated boxes drops to the following line. Those reference-only differences account for all 1,528 mismatched pixels (828 block-margin pixels and 700 float pixels). Keep candidate until block margin commit and the bounded float reference construction are supported; do not special-case the runner.

harnessBlocked.reason: This pinned CSS2 visual case verifies that list-style:square produces a square marker, but it has neither rel=match nor testharness assertions. The subset runner cannot execute self-verifying visual tests unchanged, and the case does not exercise list-style-position:inside or first-line participation. Keep it harness-blocked and use the independent local marker-pixel gate for this base rule.

This is the closest CSS2 visual statement of the exact rule: an inside marker participates on the first line while the following forced line starts at the principal block edge. It has no rel=match or assertions, so the current exact-match/testharness adapter cannot execute it unchanged; the runner-compatible nested-list reftest remains candidate and the minimal local reduction must carry the precise geometry claim.

This is the nearest official WPT and exactly asserts the base rule that visibility:hidden suppresses paint without removing layout boxes. It is a self-verifying visual test without rel=match, and it does not compose inherited hidden visibility with generated ::before content. Keep it harness-blocked; the product-neutral native/portable regression covers that exact composition and synchronous geometry read.

This exact upstream coverage is a self-overlaid visual test without rel=match or testharness assertions. The product-neutral pixel spike now covers the primitive; promote after the subset runner can evaluate self-verifying visual tests.

This is the nearest official WPT for the base shorthand rule: it checks one-value expansion and a three-value horizontal/two-value vertical ellipse. It is a self-overlaid visual test without rel=match, and HtmlML's Avalonia projection still models only one scalar per corner, so elliptical vertical paint is not claimed. WPT does not currently combine this rule with the connected-component composition of var()-substitution plus additive calc(max()) used values; the native/portable raster spike covers that exact composition.
Requires WPT check-layout-th.js support and flexbox support styles; add as the next adapter facility.

excluded.reason: Not observed in the supported connected-component interaction corpus.
Not observed in the supported connected-component interaction corpus.
Outside the local trusted chart-host profile.
Only the controls and input contracts exercised by the connected-component corpus are in scope.
The conformance lane is deterministic and headless; real interactions remain in application-owned parity probes.
